name: IPTV Checker
description: A Dispatcharr Plugin that goes through a playlist to check IPTV channels
author: PiratesIRC

fields.label: 📥 Group Selection
fields.description: Which Dispatcharr groups to check and whether to include backup streams.

fields.label: 📂 Group(s) to Check (comma-separated, wildcards supported)
fields.help_text: The name of the Dispatcharr Channel Group(s) to check. Supports wildcards: US-* matches US-Movies, US-Sports, etc. Leave blank to check all groups.

fields.label: 🔄 Check Alternative Streams
fields.help_text: Check all alternative/backup streams for each channel in addition to the primary stream. This will significantly increase check time.

fields.label: 👁️ Only Check Visible Channels
fields.help_text: Only check channels that are enabled (visible) in at least one Channel Profile. Channels disabled in every profile (or not assigned to any profile) are skipped. Useful for ignoring hidden/archived channels.

fields.label: 🔍 Check Behavior
fields.description: Timeouts, retries, concurrency, cooldown, and ffprobe analysis flags. Keep worker count below your provider's concurrent-connection limit.

fields.label: ⏱️ Connection Timeout (seconds)
fields.help_text: Network connection timeout. Use for detecting dead streams that refuse connections. Default: 10

fields.label: 🔍 Probe Timeout (seconds)
fields.help_text: Maximum time to wait for stream to start delivering data after connection. Increase this for streams with slow startup (5+ seconds). Default: 20

fields.label: 🔄 Dead Connection Retries
fields.help_text: Number of times to retry checking a stream if it appears to be dead. Default: 3

fields.label: ⚡ Enable Parallel Stream Checking
fields.help_text: Check multiple streams simultaneously for significantly faster processing. Recommended for large channel lists.

fields.label: 👷 Number of Parallel Workers
fields.help_text: Number of streams to check simultaneously when parallel checking is enabled. Default: 2. Higher values = faster but more resource-intensive. Keep BELOW your provider's max concurrent connection limit.

fields.label: ⏳ Per-Stream Cooldown (seconds)
fields.help_text: Cooldown each worker waits after finishing a stream check, before picking up the next. Prevents provider rate-limiting / slot-reuse errors when your account has a concurrent-connection limit. Default: 2. Retry passes use 3x this value.

fields.label: 🔍 FFprobe Analysis Flags
fields.help_text: Comma-separated ffprobe flags for stream analysis. Options: -show_streams (basic validation), -show_packets (per-packet data; required for video bitrate calculation on live MPEG-TS streams), -loglevel error (errors only). Default: -show_streams,-show_packets,-loglevel error. Note: do not add -show_frames — when both -show_frames and -show_packets are passed, ffprobe returns a combined packets_and_frames array instead of separate packets[], which prevents the bitrate fallback from running.

fields.label: ⏱️ FFprobe Analysis Duration (seconds)
fields.help_text: Duration to analyze stream when using -show_frames or -show_packets. Longer duration = more accurate bitrate/GOP analysis but slower checks. Probes that capture fewer than 30 video packets leave video_bitrate unset, so 8s gives slow-start streams enough room to clear the threshold. Default: 8 seconds

fields.label: ⏼ Streamlink-Only Hosts (skip ffprobe)
fields.help_text: Comma-separated host suffixes that ffprobe cannot validate (served via Streamlink). Streams on these hosts are marked 'Skipped' instead of 'Dead' so rename/move/delete actions leave them alone. Use full domains (e.g. 'youtube.com', not 'youtube'). Leave blank to use the defaults.

fields.label: ✏️ Dead Channel Handling
fields.description: How to rename and relocate channels that fail every stream check.

fields.label: 💀 Dead Channel Rename Format
fields.help_text: Format for renaming dead channels. Use {name} as placeholder for the original channel name. Examples: '[DEAD] {name}', '{name} [DEAD]', '[X] {name} [DEAD]'

fields.label: ⚰️ Move Dead Channels to Group
fields.help_text: Enter the name for the group to move dead channels into.

fields.label: 🐌 Low Framerate Handling
fields.description: How to rename and relocate channels whose streams deliver under 30 fps.

fields.label: 🐌 Low Framerate Rename Format - Less than 30fps
fields.help_text: Format for renaming low framerate channels. Use {name} as placeholder for the original channel name.

fields.label: 📁 Move Low Framerate Channels to Group
fields.help_text: Enter the name for the group to move low framerate channels into.

fields.label: 🎬 Format Suffixes
fields.description: Append a resolution tag (e.g. [HD], [FHD]) to channel names based on probed format.

fields.label: 🎬 Add Video Format Suffixes - [UHD], [FHD], [HD], [SD], [Unknown]
fields.help_text: A comma-separated list of formats to add as a suffix (e.g., [HD]) to channel names.

fields.label: 🔗 Webhook
fields.description: Optional HTTP POST notification after every stream check completes.

fields.label: 🔗 Webhook URL
fields.help_text: URL to send an HTTP POST request to after stream checks complete. Payload includes check summary (total, alive, dead counts). Leave blank to disable.

fields.label: ⏰ Scheduling & Automation
fields.description: Run checks on a cron schedule and optionally apply rename / move / delete / webhook actions automatically afterwards.

fields.label: ⏰ Scheduled Check Times (Cron Format)
fields.help_text: Comma-separated cron expressions. Format: 'minute hour day month weekday' (weekday: 0=Sunday, 6=Saturday). Examples: '0 4 * * *' (daily at 4 AM), '0 3 * * 0' (Sundays at 3 AM), '0 2 */2 * *' (every 2 days at 2 AM). Leave blank to disable scheduling. 💾 After editing this field, click "💾 Save Schedule" below to apply the new schedule.

fields.label: 🌍 Scheduler Timezone
fields.help_text: Timezone for scheduled checks. All IANA timezone names are supported.

fields.label: 🪟 Use Windowed Schedule (run only during a time window)
fields.help_text: When on, your Scheduled Times above mark the START of a run window. The check runs until the configured end-time / duration, then halts cleanly between streams. The next time the window opens it RESUMES from the same point (channels already checked are skipped). Group selection, alternative-stream and visible-only settings are read fresh at each window fire.

fields.label: 🛑 Window End Mode
fields.help_text: Choose how the window ends. Duration counts forward from the cron-fire time. Time mode supports past-midnight wrap (e.g. start 22:00, end 02:00).

fields.label: ⏳ Window Duration (hours)
fields.help_text: Used when Window End Mode = duration. Decimals allowed (e.g. 3.5). Default: 4 hours.

fields.label: 🕓 Window End Time (HH:MM, scheduler timezone)
fields.help_text: Used when Window End Mode = time. 24-hour format in the Scheduler Timezone above. If earlier than the start time, the window is treated as wrapping past midnight.

fields.label: 💾 Export CSV for Scheduled Checks
fields.help_text: Automatically export results to CSV after scheduled checks complete.

fields.label: 💀 Rename Dead Channels After Scheduled Checks
fields.help_text: Automatically rename dead channels after scheduled checks complete.

fields.label: 🐌 Rename Low Framerate Channels After Scheduled Checks
fields.help_text: Automatically rename low framerate channels after scheduled checks complete.

fields.label: 🎬 Add Video Format Suffix After Scheduled Checks
fields.help_text: Automatically add video format suffixes to channels after scheduled checks complete.

fields.label: ⚰️ Move Dead Channels After Scheduled Checks
fields.help_text: Automatically move dead channels to the configured group after scheduled checks complete.

fields.label: 📁 Move Low Framerate Channels After Scheduled Checks
fields.help_text: Automatically move low framerate channels to the configured group after scheduled checks complete.

fields.label: 🗑️ Delete Dead Channels After Scheduled Checks
fields.help_text: WARNING: This will PERMANENTLY DELETE dead channels from Dispatcharr after scheduled checks. This action is IRREVERSIBLE. Deleted channels and their stream associations cannot be recovered. The 'Auto-Delete Confirmation' field must also contain the word DELETE for this to take effect.

fields.label: ⚠️ Auto-Delete Confirmation (type DELETE to enable)
fields.help_text: SAFETY GATE: You must type the word DELETE (all caps) in this field to enable auto-deletion. This prevents accidental deletion of channels. If this field does not contain exactly DELETE, the delete function will refuse to run even if the toggle is enabled.

fields.label: 🔗 Send Webhook Notification After Scheduled Checks
fields.help_text: Send an HTTP POST notification to the Webhook URL above after each scheduled check finishes. The payload includes total, alive, and dead channel counts. Useful for alerting Discord, Slack, or other external services. Requires a Webhook URL to be configured.

fields.label: ⚙️ Advanced
fields.description: Paths and low-level overrides. Change only if your environment differs from the Dispatcharr defaults.

fields.label: 📍 FFprobe Path
fields.help_text: Full path to the ffprobe executable. Default: /usr/local/bin/ffprobe (Dispatcharr's default location)

actions.label: ✅ Validate Settings
actions.description: Validate all plugin settings (database connectivity, groups, etc.).

actions.label: 📅 Update Schedule
actions.description: Apply the current schedule settings. If scheduled times are empty, the schedule will be cleared and scheduler will be stopped.

actions.label: 🔄 Reset Window Progress
actions.description: Clear the windowed-schedule resume state. The next window will start fresh from the full channel list instead of resuming where the last window left off.

actions.label: 🔍 Check Scheduler Status
actions.description: Display scheduler thread status and diagnostic information.

actions.label: 📥 Load Group(s)
actions.description: Load channels from the specified Dispatcharr group(s) (or all groups if blank).

actions.label: ▶️ Start Stream Check
actions.description: Start checking stream status and quality for all loaded channels.

actions.label: 📊 View Check Progress
actions.description: View the current progress and ETA of the running stream check.

actions.label: 🛑 Cancel Stream Check
actions.description: Cancel the currently running stream check.

confirm.message: Cancel the in-progress stream check? Streams already probed are kept; queued and in-flight streams are aborted (ffprobe currently running may take a few seconds to exit).

actions.label: 📋 View Last Results
actions.description: View summary of the last completed stream check.

actions.label: ✏️ Rename Dead Channels
actions.description: Rename all channels marked as Dead in the last check using the configured rename format.

confirm.message: This will rename dead channels. This action is irreversible. Continue?

actions.label: ⚰️ Move Dead Channels to Group
actions.description: Moves all channels marked as Dead in the last check to the specified group.

confirm.message: This will move dead channels to the configured group. This action is irreversible. Continue?

actions.label: 🐌 Rename Low Framerate Channels
actions.description: Rename channels with streams under 30fps using the configured rename format.

confirm.message: This will rename low framerate channels. This action is irreversible. Continue?

actions.label: 📁 Move Low Framerate Channels
actions.description: Moves channels with streams under 30fps to the specified group.

confirm.message: This will move low framerate channels to the configured group. This action is irreversible. Continue?

actions.label: 🎬 Add Video Format Suffix
actions.description: Adds a format suffix like [HD] or [FHD] to alive channel names.

confirm.message: This will rename channels based on the last check. This action is irreversible. Continue?

actions.label: 📊 View Results Table
actions.description: Display detailed results in table format. (Copy/paste into text editor for better formatting.)

actions.label: 💾 Export Results to CSV
actions.description: Export the last check results to a CSV file. Will be saved in Docker container: /data/exports/

actions.label: 🧹 Cleanup Orphaned Tasks
actions.description: Remove any orphaned Celery periodic tasks from old plugin versions.

actions.label: 🗑️ Clear CSV Exports
actions.description: Delete all CSV export files created by this plugin.

confirm.message: This will delete all CSV files in /data/exports/. This action cannot be undone. Continue?

actions.label: 🗑️ Delete Dead Channels
actions.description: Permanently delete all channels marked as Dead in the last check from the database.

confirm.message: WARNING: This will PERMANENTLY DELETE dead channels from the database. This action CANNOT be undone. The 'Auto-Delete Confirmation' field must contain DELETE for this to work. Continue?
